run_summary: Portfolio is fully in cash (10284.22 USD) with no open positions. No buy-eligible discoveries flagged in the last 7 days. Track record shows strong 80% hit rate on OVERDONE calls, but only 50% confidence at conf3 level—suggests recent conf3 picks were weaker than conf2 ones. Prudent to wait for clearer signals.

entries.name: AAON, Inc.
entries.thesis: A 31% single-day move on earnings + 8.01 'other events' often signals more than just a beat — possibilities include a major data-center/AI cooling contract win, raised guide, or strategic announcement bundled with the print. AAON's exposure to data-center liquid cooling makes a contract-win narrative highly plausible given the AI capex tape.
entries.what_kills: 8.01 turns out to be routine (dividend, buyback) and the move is purely a short squeeze on a guide-raise — in that case 31% is hard to sustain without follow-through fundamentals.

entries.name: Installed Building Products
entries.thesis: IBP is a pure-play single-family construction installer — a 28% gap on Q1 likely reflects a real volume/margin miss compounded by housing-cycle deterioration, but magnitude on a profitable cash-generative installer feels stretched. Sympathy with WHR -12%, SGI -10% suggests a broader housing/durables read-through.
entries.what_kills: Miss is one-time (weather, project timing) with reaffirmed full-year guide — then move is an overreaction in a name with strong FCF and buybacks.

entries.name: Planet Fitness
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings + guide cut + canceled price hike
entries.thesis: Slashing guidance AND canceling a planned price increase is a double-negative: it confirms both demand softness and management's loss of confidence in pricing power. That combination justifies severe re-rating in a unit-growth franchise model where pricing was a key thesis component. 31% is harsh but mechanism is clearly identified.
entries.what_kills: Management frames cancelation as goodwill/timing rather than demand; member counts actually growing — would suggest overreaction.

entries.name: H&R Block
entries.thesis: HRB's fiscal Q3 captures the bulk of tax-season revenue. A 24% gap typically requires both a beat and a guide raise or capital-return announcement. Counter-trend to broader consumer cyclical weakness suggests company-specific positive catalyst.
entries.what_kills: Beat is purely a one-time tax-prep volume spike with no margin/buyback follow-through.

entries.name: Coca-Cola Consolidated
entries.thesis: COKE has rallied massively over recent years on margin expansion thesis; a 16% gap on a bottler is unusual and likely reflects margin compression (PET, aluminum, freight) plus volume softness. Low-float dynamics can amplify; possible overshoot but bottler margin reversals tend to persist multiple quarters.
entries.what_kills: One-time hurricane/weather impact with margin recovery in Q2 commentary.

entries.name: Whirlpool
entries.thesis: WHR is heavily levered to housing turnover and consumer durables; with IBP -28% and SGI -10% same day, this is a broader housing-cycle theme rather than an isolated event. Whirlpool also carries dividend-cut risk given high leverage and weak FCF — 12% drop is consistent with that overhang.
entries.what_kills: Tariff-relief commentary or cost actions offsetting volume — though current Trump EU tariff post argues against this.

entries.name: Lumen Technologies
entries.thesis: Lumen reported May 5; today's additional 8.01 'other events' filing combined with continued slide suggests follow-on disclosure (debt action, customer loss, AI-connectivity contract update). Volume only 1.29x average is notable — selling pressure isn't massive, suggesting drift rather than capitulation.
entries.what_kills: 8.01 is benign (annual meeting, routine matter) — then move is sympathy with telco/leveraged names.

entries.name: Paycom Software
entries.thesis: PAYC has been a heavily-shorted disappointer over the last two years. A 10% rally on earnings with moderate volume in a risk-off tape is a healthy beat — possibly with stabilization in Beti automation cannibalization narrative that has plagued the stock. Counter-tape strength is meaningful.
entries.what_kills: Beat was driven by float income or one-time items rather than core SaaS growth.

entries.name: DXP Enterprises
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed
entries.thesis: Empty catalyst_signals and only 1.65x volume on a 17% drop is unusual — could be a delayed earnings reaction, a private placement leak, or an industrial-distribution sympathy move (with XLI -1.6% and oil/energy tape weak, DXPE's energy-services exposure may be the driver). Without a filing or news, call this honestly: don't know.
entries.what_kills: Stock recovers on no news — confirms it was thin-trading dislocation.

entries.name: Middleby Corporation
entries.thesis: MIDD filed a 7.01 Reg FD on May 4 — three days before the earnings 8-K — which often precedes strategic announcements (spin-off update, segment sale). Combined with the 11% rally, possible the company advanced a previously-discussed Food Processing or Residential spin-off plan.
entries.what_kills: May 4 8-K turns out to be investor day deck only, with no spin commitment.

entries.name: Primoris Services Corporation
entries.thesis: Earnings + officer departure (5.02) + 'other events' combined — magnitude suggests guidance cut, project writedown, or CFO/CEO exit on top of miss. A 50% single-day drop in an engineering & construction name with $5.5B cap implies the market is pricing material backlog/contract impairment, not just a quarterly miss.
entries.what_kills: If 8-K shows planned CEO retirement with successor named and earnings only modestly missed, then -50% is overdone. If it shows restatement risk or project impairment of major scale, then move is justified or even insufficient.

entries.name: TransMedics Group
entries.thesis: Clean earnings 8-K only (2.02 + 9.01) — no officer departure or restructuring flagged. -23% on an earnings print with no other red-flag items typically means revenue miss or guide-down on growth deceleration (TMDX has historically traded at growth multiples on OCS organ transport adoption).
entries.what_kills: If guide was reaffirmed and miss was modest, -23% is overdone and likely a setup for bounce. If full-year guide cut materially or aviation segment showed structural problems, move is justified.

entries.name: Veeco Instruments
entries.thesis: Semi-equipment beat into a tape where SMH +5.2% and TSM +6.4% are confirming AI-capex acceleration. Veeco leverages advanced packaging (laser annealing, MOCVD) — direct beneficiary of TSM/leading-edge capex commentary.
entries.what_kills: If guide was in-line and move is purely sympathy/short-cover, fade risk after sector cools

entries.name: Axcelis Technologies
entries.catalyst: Sector sympathy + pre-earnings positioning (reports today after close)
entries.thesis: ACLS reports AFTER hours TODAY (in ~10 hours) — this is pre-positioning into earnings inside a +5% semi-equipment tape. 5.07 8-K is just shareholder vote results, not catalyst. The 22% pre-earnings rip is mostly sympathy with VECO/SMH and short squeeze.
entries.what_kills: Strong beat-and-raise tonight justifies the move; in-line print likely fades 5-10% as the sympathy premium unwinds

entries.name: Kyndryl Holdings
entries.thesis: 8-K has 2.05 (restructuring/exit costs) alongside earnings — explicit restructuring charge announcement. -11% reasonable for restructuring + likely soft guide in IT services with EU cloud-restriction headlines (per news) creating overhang on US IT services exposure to European clients.
entries.what_kills: If restructuring is targeted at low-margin contracts and signings/FCF guide raised, -11% is overdone

entries.name: Comstock Resources
entries.thesis: Earnings drop compounded by sector wipeout (XLE -4.1%) on oil pullback. CRK is a Haynesville natgas pure-play, so oil tape isn't the direct driver — earnings/guide must have disappointed on capex or production. Sector tape is amplifying.
entries.what_kills: If production beat and natgas peers also down 5%+, sympathy effect explains most of move

entries.name: Clean Harbors
entries.thesis: Clean earnings 8-K only. -8% with 4x volume on a +2.6% XLI day is meaningful underperformance. Likely guide miss on environmental services or PFAS-related cost commentary. Worth investigating because CLH typically trades stable.
entries.what_kills: If guide was reaffirmed and miss small, -8% is overdone for this quality compounder

entries.name: GEO Group
entries.thesis: Private prison/detention operator earnings beat in current administration's immigration-enforcement environment — operating leverage on detention bed contracts is the bull thesis. +21% suggests material guide raise on ICE contract expansion.
entries.what_kills: Beat driven by one-time items rather than contract expansion would mean rally fades

entries.name: Elanco Animal Health
entries.thesis: Earnings beat on a turnaround story — ELAN has been working through deleveraging and pet health product launches (Credelio Quattro, Zenrelia)
entries.what_kills: If guidance only nudged and beat was FX-driven, gains may fade

entries.name: California Resources
entries.thesis: Earnings combined with sector-wide energy selloff (XLE -4.1%) — double headwind from idiosyncratic print and sector
entries.what_kills: If XLE rebounds and CRC underperforms, idiosyncratic story is worse than appears; if CRC rebounds with sector, sympathy component dominated

entries.name: Freshpet
entries.thesis: Earnings disappointment continuing 5-day -17% slide — growth deceleration narrative for the high-multiple pet food story
entries.what_kills: If guidance was maintained and weakness is one-quarter weather/promotional, -17% 5-day is overdone

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings
entries.catalyst: Probable earnings release - need to verify
entries.thesis: Likely earnings beat / AI inference workload narrative; +61% over 5 days suggests possible guide raise or M&A speculation
entries.what_kills: If move is purely multiple expansion without fundamental beat, or if it's M&A rumor that fails to materialize

entries.name: IPG Photonics
entries.catalyst: Likely Q1 earnings miss or weak Q2 guide
entries.thesis: Earnings disappointment / weak guidance in industrial laser business; sector has structural headwinds from Chinese competition
entries.what_kills: Strong cash position and stabilizing book-to-bill could make this an oversold value play

entries.name: Cytokinetics
entries.catalyst: Need to identify - possible FDA AdCom date, label, or buyout chatter
entries.thesis: Likely binary catalyst - aficamten regulatory/clinical update, FDA action, or partnership/M&A speculation
entries.what_kills: If pure speculation without confirmable catalyst, fade risk is high after 27% 5-day move

entries.name: OSI Systems
entries.thesis: Likely earnings disappointment or contract/order pushout in security division (airport/border scanning). Stock had run hard previously
entries.what_kills: If backlog still growing and miss is timing-related, drop may be overdone

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.catalyst: Likely earnings release with disappointing guide
entries.thesis: Notable divergence vs. SMH +3.14% - selling on its own catalyst (likely earnings/guide) while semicap peers rally. Possible misread if guide is conservative but data center power story intact
entries.what_kills: If data center power demand was raised but semi was weak, mix could be misread positively

entries.name: Adeia Inc.
entries.thesis: IP licensing company - drop likely tied to license renewal failure, lost litigation ruling, or weak earnings/guidance
entries.what_kills: Lumpy licensing revenue is normal - if pipeline of negotiations intact, drop overdone

entries.name: GXO Logistics
entries.thesis: Bounce after -12% 5-day decline; likely earnings beat or strategic update. Sympathy with PRIM/industrials strength possible
entries.what_kills: Bounce within larger downtrend if guide was lowered

entries.name: Everus Construction
entries.catalyst: Earnings beat / data center backlog growth
entries.thesis: Data center / electrical infrastructure buildout beneficiary; +23% 5-day suggests sustained re-rating with PRIM, POWL also strong (+9% each)
entries.what_kills: If backlog growth decelerates or hyperscaler capex commentary turns cautious

entries.name: GameStop Corporation
entries.catalyst: GameStop surprise takeover bid for eBay at $125/share; Cohen CNBC interview poorly received
entries.thesis: Acquirer drawdown on surprise large M&A bid (GME bidding $55.5B for eBay) - prediction markets give only 15-25% odds of completion. Cash-and-stock structure dilutive risk plus combative CEO interview added uncertainty.
entries.what_kills: EBAY board engagement, financing commitments disclosed, or prediction market odds rising materially above 30%

entries.name: C.H. Robinson Worldwide
entries.catalyst: Amazon announces opening supply chain network to outside businesses; UPS/FedEx also down
entries.thesis: Sympathy/competitive selling on Amazon opening logistics network to third parties. CHRW is a 3PL/freight broker, so Amazon-as-competitor narrative directly applies, but Amazon logistics-as-a-service has been telegraphed for years and CHRW's brokerage model differs from parcel. -14% over 5 days suggests cumulative damage may be overdone if fundamentals unchanged.
entries.what_kills: Amazon announcement explicitly includes truckload brokerage at scale, or CHRW issues guidance cut citing customer losses

entries.name: Saia, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Amazon logistics news driving freight sector weakness; XLI -1.14%
entries.thesis: Sympathy selling within freight on Amazon logistics news, but volume_multiple is only 1.05 - move on average volume suggests gradual, not panic. SAIA is LTL trucking, even further from Amazon's current parcel network than CHRW. The -8% with no above-average volume is a warning that algo/sector sweep is doing the selling.
entries.what_kills: SAIA-specific negative news (downgrade, guidance cut, customer loss), or evidence Amazon is entering LTL

entries.name: Axsome Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Unknown specific catalyst; XBI +2.34% provides sector tailwind but stock outperforming sector materially
entries.thesis: +19% over 5 days with elevated volume into a strong XBI day suggests pipeline/data catalyst. No specific headline in feed - check for clinical trial readouts, label expansion, or M&A speculation given biotech bid tone.
entries.what_kills: No identifiable catalyst found - if move is purely momentum/sympathy it's vulnerable to mean reversion

entries.name: UFP Industries
entries.catalyst: Not identified in news feed; possible earnings or housing data driver
entries.thesis: -16% over 5 days with elevated volume in a weak XLB tape (-1.38%) suggests company-specific headwind beyond sector. Lumber/wood production exposed to housing slowdown. Could be earnings reaction or guidance cut not in feed.
entries.what_kills: Confirmation of guidance cut or weak earnings would shift to LIKELY RATIONAL

entries.name: Alaska Air Group
entries.catalyst: Oil +to $114 on Iran-UAE attacks; offset partially by Spirit collapse benefiting survivors
entries.thesis: Airlines hit by oil at 4-year high ($114) - jet fuel cost pressure. Counter-narrative: Spirit demise news was framed as positive for rival pricing power. ALK -8.6% over 5 days suggests oil/Iran fears dominating over Spirit-benefit story.
entries.what_kills: Oil retreats below $100 quickly; or ALK issues unit-revenue update reflecting Spirit benefit

entries.name: Merit Medical Systems
entries.catalyst: Unknown from data provided — likely Q1 earnings or guidance
entries.thesis: Single-day -9% on 3.5x volume with no headline in the provided news set; likely earnings-driven given mid-cap medtech reporting season, but catalyst not confirmed in feed
entries.what_kills: If earnings beat with raised guide and the drop was a sympathy reaction or technical breakdown, the move is likely overdone; if guide was materially cut, -9% may be rational

entries.name: Builders FirstSource
entries.catalyst: Sector de-rating; possibly tied to homebuilder/lumber demand commentary, mortgage rate stickiness, or a peer earnings miss not in the feed
entries.thesis: Housing/building-products group selling off together (BLDR -4.3%, UFPI -4.8%, both down ~10-16% over 5 days) — likely reflects weakening housing data or peer earnings disappointment cascading through the supply chain
entries.what_kills: Confirmation of a structural demand reset (sustained homebuilder guide-downs) would make -16% over 5 days insufficient rather than overdone

entries.name: AeroVironment
entries.catalyst: Iran de-escalation rhetoric reducing perceived urgency of unmanned systems orders
entries.thesis: Defense sector weak (ITA -1.05%) plus Trump signaling Iran 'hostilities terminated' — peace narrative compresses near-term defense order expectations. Drone names like AVAV had run hard on Ukraine/Iran demand
entries.what_kills: A confirmed contract loss or earnings miss would shift this to LIKELY RATIONAL
